funding.summary: BorgWarner Inc. announced a $100 million investment to expand its Hendersonville, North Carolina operations, adding 378 jobs.
funding.funding_type: Company Investment with Government Grant Support
funding.use_of_proceeds: Vertical integration of operations and production of critical components, expanding the Hendersonville site by 140,000 square feet for new production infrastructure.
funding.why_it_matters: This expansion for new production infrastructure will drive demand for various lab supplies, reagents, consumables, and scientific equipment for quality control and R&D.

funding.summary: Novartis announced an additional $220 million investment to build a new facility in Morrisville, North Carolina, focused on active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) manufacturing.
funding.use_of_proceeds: New facility for active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) manufacturing for solid dosage tablets, capsules, and RNA therapeutics, strengthening end-to-end U.S. production capability.
funding.why_it_matters: The new API manufacturing facility will require significant lab supplies, reagents, consumables, and scientific equipment for R&D, quality control, and production processes.

ai_summary: BorgWarner and Novartis are making significant industrial and pharmaceutical investments in North Carolina. This regional growth will increase demand for lab supplies, equipment, and services for R&D and quality control. For a lab supply sales team, this signals new opportunities within Carolina Components and across the broader industrial and pharma market.
